addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Memo: Chat-Log Capture & Cost Reconstruction (JJK)
2	
3	*2026-06-15. Provenance for a design session (Brad + Claude Opus 4.8). Informs the
4	₣BD chat-capture paces — (1) store decision + first-time backfill, (2) auto-recurring
5	capture — and a deferred cost-analytics pace. No source changed this session; the
6	working prototypes were throwaway jq+bash.*
7	
8	## Why
9	
10	Claude Code transcripts live at `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/*.jsonl`. They are
11	machine-local and GC-pruned (a `~/.claude/.last-cleanup` runs periodically), so the
12	chat record is a perishable asset: you cannot analyze a conversation that has already
13	been silently reaped. JJK should capture these logs into the project before they
14	disappear; **cost and other analytics ride on the captured corpus later** — capture
15	first, analytics after, because capture is the part that races the clock.
16	
17	## Empirical findings (this repo, measured 2026-06-15)
18	
19	### Cost is reconstructable, not stored
20	
21	- Transcripts store per-assistant-message **token usage + model**, never a dollar
22	  cost. `/usage`'s cost is *computed*: tokens × public per-token rates. (No `costUSD`
23	  field in current Claude Code transcripts; older versions had one — format drift.)
24	- `message.usage` fields: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`,
25	  `cache_creation_input_tokens` (+ a `cache_creation.ephemeral_1h/_5m` split), plus
26	  `message.model`.
27	- Validated: a `/usage`-reported **$6.91** session reconstructs to **$6.89** — the
28	  gap is rounding in the displayed token figures. Method is sound.
29	
30	### Rates (per 1M tokens, list price; cache rates derived from the input rate)
31	
32	| Model | Input | Output |
33	|---|---|---|
34	| opus 4.8 / 4.7 / 4.6 | $5 | $25 |
35	| sonnet 4.6 | $3 | $15 |
36	| haiku 4.5 | $1 | $5 |
37	| fable 5 | $10 | $50 |
38	
39	Cache read = 0.1× input; cache write 5m TTL = 1.25× input; 1h TTL = 2× input (Claude
40	Code writes 1h). Model `<synthetic>` = $0 (locally-injected turns, not billed).
41	
42	### Whole-project totals
43	
44	- **~$6,839** of API-equivalent value across **63 days** (deduped). On the
45	  subscription plan this is the shadow price, not what was paid.
46	- By model: opus-4-7 dominant (~$4,089), then opus-4-8 (~$1,121), opus-4-6 (~$830),
47	  fable-5 (~$796), sonnet-4-6 (~$2).
48	- **Dedup is load-bearing.** ~43,608 of ~89,516 assistant records are forked/resumed
49	  copies sharing the same API `message.id` but **distinct transcript `uuid`s** (a fork
50	  re-stamps uuids). Dedup **by `message.id`** in the cost parser bills each generation
51	  once; a naive sum nearly doubles the total. Git delta handles the *storage*
52	  redundancy of the verbatim copies on its own.
53	
54	### Byte mass (839 MB raw for this project)
55	
56	| Bucket | ~MB | What |
57	|---|---|---|
58	| tool_result | 102 | file reads, grep/glob, command output |
59	| tool_use | 26 | tool inputs incl. Write/Edit payloads |
60	| other_records | 18 | per-session injected context boilerplate |
61	| assistant_text | 16 | the assistant's prose |
62	| user_text | 4 | operator messages |
63	| thinking | ~0 | omitted/empty on 4.8/4.7 |
64	| embedded images | 0 | none present |
65	
66	The conversation you'd actually study (prose) ≈ **20 MB**; the rest is machinery,
67	most of it recoverable from git (file reads/writes) or compressible (verbatim forks).
68	
69	## Cinched decisions
70	
71	- **Home:** JJK / Rust (`jjx`).
72	- **Attribution is in-band.** Every jjx call is recorded in the transcript as a
73	  `tool_use` carrying its coronet/firemark + officium, interleaved with billed turns
74	  in timestamp order. So heat/pace attribution is a single forward pass over one
75	  session's transcript — no external join, concurrency-safe across multiple officia.
76	  (Confirmed empirically: e.g. `jjx_record ₢A6AAC` under officium `☉260413-1019`.)
77	- **Wholesale capture; the boundary is per-mechanism.** No relevance filtering — every
78	  session in each source dir. The deliberate one-time backfill is an operator-chosen
79	  *consolidation* (alpha + beta + cerebro-alpha into one store) — no accidental bleed in a
80	  hand-run act. The "don't absorb another repo's chats" boundary governs the **automatic
81	  recurring** mechanism, which stays scoped to the current project's transcript dir.
82	- **Never-forget but update.** Additive sync: pick up new session files and the growth
83	  of existing ones; never delete from the store even when the source disappears. Git
84	  history carries the "never forget."
85	- **Compression is git's own zlib + delta.** No external compressor. Supply-chain
86	  reasoning: a pinned/checksummed Cargo crate beats an *ambient, unpinned* system
87	  binary (`zstd` CLI), but git-zlib adds **zero** new trust surface and is the tightest
88	  exposure management — and for a rarely-read, git-delta-friendly archive the ratio
89	  gain of zstd did not justify the added C/FFI/build surface.
90	- **Triggers:** mount + groom + muster (the frequent orientation ceremonies; more
91	  frequent than wrap, which is rare under chat daisy-chaining).
92	- **Deferred:** cost accounting, attribution, analytics — later paces on the captured
93	  corpus.
94	
95	## Storage finding (corrects a prior assumption)
96	
97	The store does **not** belong in `vov_veiled/`. From the delivery-path investigation:
98	
99	- `vov_veiled/` is **excluded from the parcel** (`Tools/vok/vof/src/vofr_release.rs`
100	  skips veiled paths; the `rbk-prep-release` ceremony `git rm`s them).
101	- Delivery is a **nuclear install**: `Tools/vok/vof/src/vofe_emplace.rs`
102	  `remove_dir_all`s each `Tools/{kit}/` and rebuilds from the parcel. So a store under
103	  `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/` would be **both absent on consumers and destroyed on
104	  re-delivery**.
105	- `.claude/jjm/` is the right home: emplace only touches `Tools/{kit}` + `.vvk`, never
106	  `.claude/`; jjm is committed to git and consumer-local (not in the parcel → not
107	  distributed). The store lives **alongside gallops**, e.g. `.claude/jjm/chat-archive/`.
108	- Defensive `mkdir -p` on first write; commit a `.gitkeep` so the dir exists on fresh
109	  clones.
110	
111	## Open thread: the state gestalt (not resolved)
112	
113	There are **two "never ships" categories, and only one is named:**
114	
115	- **veiled** = kit-private *source* — proprietary authored content under `Tools/{kit}/`,
116	  reviewed as design, *replaceable* (wiped + rebuilt on re-emplace).
117	- **jjm** = project-local *state* — generated runtime data under `.claude/`, must
118	  *survive* updates, scoped *per-project* not per-kit.
119	
120	jjm survives re-delivery today only because emplace's deletion scope happens to be
121	narrow — an **emergent** guarantee, not a **stated** one. That is the "murkiness"
122	next to veiled's clean rule. Open question: name the state-gestalt as a first-class
123	rule (release never ships it, emplace never touches it) so jjm's protection becomes a
124	contract parallel to veiled's. **Gallops should not move into veiled** — lifecycle
125	(wiped on re-emplace), category (source vs state), and scope (per-kit vs per-project)
126	all mismatch. Discussion ongoing.
127	
128	## Follow-ups
129	
130	- **Two capture paces slated** into ₣BD: ₢BDAAX (store + first-time backfill of
131	  local-alpha + local-beta + cerebro-alpha) before ₢BDAAK, then ₢BDAAY (auto-recurring at
132	  mount/groom/muster). Store pinned "alongside gallops" so it tracks that category
133	  regardless of the gestalt outcome.
134	- **Beta's _ongoing_ capture** is the real open question for the recurring mechanism:
135	  does alpha's auto-capture also sweep beta, or does beta self-capture? The one-time
136	  backfill rescues beta's _existing_ history into the store regardless; only beta's
137	  _future_ turns on this.
138	- **Cost-analytics pace** deferred until the corpus is captured.
139	- **State-gestalt naming** — open (above).
140	
141	## Prototype residue
142	
143	Throwaway jq + bash prototypes from this session lived in `/tmp` (`/tmp/rbcost.jq`,
144	`/tmp/breakdown.jq`) — proof-of-method only, never repo artifacts. The real
145	implementation is Rust in `jjx`.
146

|---|---|---|

| sonnet 4.6 | $3 | $15 |

| fable 5 | $10 | $50 |

Code writes 1h). Model `<synthetic>` = $0 (locally-injected turns, not billed).

|---|---|---|

Docket:
  ## Character
  Decide-then-do, but mostly do.
  The store location is settled (alongside gallops); the work is a one-time, multi-source backfill.
  The backfill can be scripted by hand — only the recurring mechanism (next pace) needs a Rust hook.
  
  ## Goal
  Perform the first-time backfill of the full Claude Code transcript history into a committed store,
  wholesale, before Claude Code's GC prunes it.
  
  ## Sources
  - Local alpha — this repo's transcript dir under the user's Claude Code projects.
  - Local beta — the rbm_beta_recipemuster transcript dir under the user's Claude Code projects.
  - Cerebro — the alpha project's transcripts on the cerebro host, alpha only, gathered over ssh.
  
  ## Cinched
  - This is a deliberate, operator-chosen consolidation: it pulls alpha, beta, and cerebro-alpha into
    one store. The "don't absorb another repo's chats" boundary governs the AUTOMATIC recurring
    mechanism (next pace), not this hand-run backfill — there is no accidental absorption here.
  - The store lives alongside gallops under `.claude/jjm/` (a new subdir), never in `vov_veiled/`.
    Rationale: emplace nuclear-wipes kit directories and the parcel excludes veiled, so a veiled store
    would be both undistributed and destroyed on re-delivery, whereas `.claude/jjm/` is untouched by
    emplace, committed, and consumer-local.
    Provenance: `Memos/memo-20260615-chat-capture-and-cost-reconstruction.md`.
  - Wholesale — every session in each source dir, no relevance filtering.
  - Compression is git's own zlib + delta; no external compressor, no new dependency.
  - The commit is scoped to the store paths, orthogonal to other officia's work.
  
  ## Mount-time decisions
  - Exact subdir name and per-source layout — namespace by source so alpha, beta, and cerebro-alpha
    cannot collide; a committed `.gitkeep`; `mkdir -p` on first write.
  - The "name the state-gestalt" question (see memo) is open but does not block.
  
  ## Done when
  - The store exists under `.claude/jjm/` and the full current transcript history from local-alpha,
    local-beta, and cerebro-alpha is committed into it.
  - The commit is scoped to store paths.

# jjezs_paddock ₣BD

## Context

Tactical heat for V4-era JJK improvements that are ready to mount — settled designs, mechanical fixes, and concrete spook repairs. Carved out of ₣Am (jjk-v5-notional) on 260507 to surface unblocked work that was hidden in the parking lot.

## Character

Fast tweaks. Bounded scope. No architectural change.

A pace lives here only if its docket is mount-ready — design settled, files identified, exit criteria concrete. If a pace grows hair (uncovered design questions, scope creep, prerequisite hunts), bump it back to ₣Am rather than letting it linger here.

## Inclusion criteria

- Single-file or small-cluster edit
- Spec change with worked-out shape
- Spook fix with clear repair
- Investigation with reproduction already known

## Exclusion criteria

- Open design questions ("which option?")
- Multi-pace dependency chains beyond what's already enrolled
- Anything that would benefit from chat-time design conversation before slating

## Origin

Restrung from ₣Am on 260507 during groom session. Candidates identified as "ready" based on docket maturity:

- AAK — clarify-furlough-verb-in-claude-context (mechanical, single file)
- AAL — capture-session-and-officium-in-git-trailer (settled in ☉260418-1002)
- AAM — size-guard-review-not-split (spook fix, clear shape)
- AAN — jjx-paddock-honor-size-limit-param (spook fix + audit)
- AAH — harden-paddock-encoding (settled; verify ₣Ag prereq at mount)
- AAO — diagnose-localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation (investigation, reproduction known)

## V4 currency

Each pace's docket was scanned for V3-era assumptions before transfer. All references current MCP surface (jjx_open, gazette, officium, current commit-pattern grammar). Prereq verification still required at mount-time for AAH (₣Ag migration completion).

## Related Heats

- ₣Am (jjk-v5-notional) — source parking lot; remaining paces stay there
- ₣Ah (jjk-v4-1-school-breeze-founding) — primary V4 development heat
- ₣An (jjk-v4-2-release-and-legacy-removal) — V4 cleanup heat

# jjezs_pace ₢BDAAX

## Character
Decide-then-do, but mostly do.
The store location is settled (alongside gallops); the work is a one-time, multi-source backfill.
The backfill can be scripted by hand — only the recurring mechanism (next pace) needs a Rust hook.

## Goal
Perform the first-time backfill of the full Claude Code transcript history into a committed store,
wholesale, before Claude Code's GC prunes it.

## Sources
- Local alpha — this repo's transcript dir under the user's Claude Code projects.
- Local beta — the rbm_beta_recipemuster transcript dir under the user's Claude Code projects.
- Cerebro — the alpha project's transcripts on the cerebro host, alpha only, gathered over ssh.

## Cinched
- This is a deliberate, operator-chosen consolidation: it pulls alpha, beta, and cerebro-alpha into
  one store. The "don't absorb another repo's chats" boundary governs the AUTOMATIC recurring
  mechanism (next pace), not this hand-run backfill — there is no accidental absorption here.
- The store lives alongside gallops under `.claude/jjm/` (a new subdir), never in `vov_veiled/`.
  Rationale: emplace nuclear-wipes kit directories and the parcel excludes veiled, so a veiled store
  would be both undistributed and destroyed on re-delivery, whereas `.claude/jjm/` is untouched by
  emplace, committed, and consumer-local.
  Provenance: `Memos/memo-20260615-chat-capture-and-cost-reconstruction.md`.
- Wholesale — every session in each source dir, no relevance filtering.
- Compression is git's own zlib + delta; no external compressor, no new dependency.
- The commit is scoped to the store paths, orthogonal to other officia's work.

## Mount-time decisions
- Exact subdir name and per-source layout — namespace by source so alpha, beta, and cerebro-alpha
  cannot collide; a committed `.gitkeep`; `mkdir -p` on first write.
- The "name the state-gestalt" question (see memo) is open but does not block.

## Done when
- The store exists under `.claude/jjm/` and the full current transcript history from local-alpha,
  local-beta, and cerebro-alpha is committed into it.
- The commit is scoped to store paths.

content.text:
Safe to proceed: `.claude/jjm/chat-archive/` is committable (jjm is tracked; only `.claude/jjm/officia/` is ignored).

Here's what's actually on disk across the five source dirs, and the layout I propose.

**Survey (~1.04 GB total, raw):**

| Source | encoded dir | content | size |
|---|---|---|---|
| local alpha | `…-rbm-alpha-recipemuster` | 1370 `.jsonl` + 605 subagent dirs + `sessions-index.json` | 841M |
| local alpha (vvc) | `…-alpha-recipemuster-Tools-vvc` | 70 `.jsonl` | 280K |
| local beta | `…-rbm-beta-recipemuster` | 261 `.jsonl` + 46 subagent dirs + `sessions-index.json` | 143M |
| local beta (vvc) | `…-beta-recipemuster-Tools-vvc` | 18 `.jsonl` | 72K |
| cerebro alpha | `-home-bhyslop-…-alpha-recipemuster` | 147 `.jsonl` + 15 subagent dirs | 53M |

Two things the docket couldn't have anticipated, both handled by the layout:
- Each local source has a **second encoded dir** — the `-Tools-vvc` subdir-cwd sibling (same project, run from a subdirectory). Preserving the encoded-dir name inside each namespace lets it sit beside the main dir without colliding.
- Beyond top-level `.jsonl`, each main dir holds **subagent transcripts** (`<session-uuid>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl`) and a `sessions-index.json`. Subagent runs are real conversation content.

**Proposed store** (`chat-archive`, the memo's example name):

```
.claude/jjm/chat-archive/
├── .gitkeep
├── local-alpha/
│   ├── -Users-bhyslop-projects-rbm-alpha-recipemuster/            (verbatim tree)
│   └── -Users-bhyslop-projects-rbm-alpha-recipemuster-Tools-vvc/
├── local-beta/
│   ├── -Users-bhyslop-projects-rbm-beta-recipemuster/
│   └── -Users-bhyslop-projects-rbm-beta-recipemuster-Tools-vvc/
└── cerebro-alpha/
    └── -home-bhyslop-projects-rbm-alpha-recipemuster/
```

One flag, not a blocker: these go into git history **verbatim and permanently** — whatever was in the chats (tokens, paths, secrets) rides along. Cinched as wholesale/no-filtering, and `.claude/jjm/` is private + undistributed, so I'll proceed unless you say otherwise.
